title: Getting Started with dagriculture
text: Registry: Defines allowable kinds nodes graph. Graph: main immutable data structure carrying nodes, edges, gates. Operations return new graph bump internal $version. Nodes & Edges: Represent units computation (data) dependencies. Gates: Explicit structural blockers attached edges, prevent downstream nodes becoming ready gate resolved. Planning: Topologically sorting tracking structural readiness nodes.

title: Getting Started with dagriculture
Getting Started with dagriculture
text: First, establish registry strictly define kind nodes allowed graphs.
code: library(dagriculture)  reg <- dagri_registry(   dagri_kind("input", output_type = "data.frame"),   dagri_kind("model"),   dagri_kind("report") )

title: Getting Started with dagriculture
text: Start empty graph, add nodes edges. dagriculture uses value semantics, every mutating function returns new updated graph instance.
code: g <- dagri_graph(reg)  g <- g |>   dagri_add_node(id = "data", kind = "input", label = "Raw Data") |>   dagri_add_node(id = "fit", kind = "model", label = "Statistical Fit") |>   dagri_add_node(id = "plot", kind = "report", label = "Final Plot") |>   dagri_add_edge(from = "data", to = "fit", id = "e_data_fit") |>   dagri_add_edge(from = "fit", to = "plot", id = "e_fit_plot")  # The internal version tracks changes g$version #> [1] 5

title: Getting Started with dagriculture
text: can place “gate” edge represent manual external block—instance, requiring human approval statistical fit proceeds.

title: Getting Started with dagriculture
text: dagriculture tracks structural readiness . execute anything know whether node computed, currently running, failed. three structural node states, set [dagri_recompute_state()], : node blocked, dagri_recompute_state() records block_reason:

title: Getting Started with dagriculture
text: Execution state consumer’s overlay, part structural model. particular, dagriculture never records: node structurally ready may still skipped consumer reusable result exists; conversely blocked node never run blocker clears. Call [dagri_recompute_state()] whenever graph structure gate statuses change, state/block_reason fields reflect current graph planning.

title: Getting Started with dagriculture
text: Use dagri_recompute_state() evaluate graph topologically determine nodes structurally ready blocked. can generate structural plan see dependencies shake . dagri_plan() derives state internally, require recomputed graph value : Planner-visible external holds can layered onto planning without mutating graph:
code: g_state <- dagri_recompute_state(g)  # The 'data' node is a root and has no blockers dagri_eligible(g_state) #> [1] "data"  # The 'fit' node is blocked by the gate; the 'plot' node is blocked because 'fit' is blocked. dagri_blocked(g_state) #> $fit #> [1] "gate" #>  #> $plot #> [1] "upstream_blocked" plan <- dagri_plan(g_state) plan$targets #> [1] "data" "fit"  "plot" plan$blocked #> $fit #> [1] "gate" #>  #> $plot #> [1] "upstream_blocked" plan$external_blocked #> named list() plan$pending_gates #> [1] "approval_gate" dagri_plan(   g_state,   external_holds = list(fit = "manual_review") )$external_blocked #> $fit #> [1] "manual_review" #>  #> $plot #> [1] "manual_review"

title: Getting Started with dagriculture
text: external condition met, can resolve gate. Computing state reveals downstream dependencies now unblocked.
code: g_unblocked <- dagri_resolve_gate(g_state, id = "approval_gate") |>   dagri_recompute_state()  # Everything is unblocked and ready for execution dagri_eligible(g_unblocked) #> [1] "data" "fit"  "plot" dagri_blocked(g_unblocked) #> named list()

title: Getting Started with dagriculture

what: Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
title: Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
text: dagriculture pure, value-oriented directed-acyclic-graph library R. manages graph structure, topological dependencies, structural validity, task planning — deliberately nothing else. Every operation takes graph value returns new graph value: mutation. Graphs plain R lists; editing verbs return modified copies, snapshots, diffs, rollbacks just variable assignments. execution. dagriculture tells run (dagri_plan()), never runs anything , holds opinions node . IO code. Graphs contain data. Node kinds can declare parameter schemas, executable closures rejected construction — graph loaded disk can never smuggle code session. Validity construction. Duplicate ids, unknown kinds, dangling edges, cycle-creating edges rejected edit time typed error taxonomy (dagri_error_*). makes safe structural core runtime layers built top. primary consumer bayesgrove, layers caching, execution, Bayesian-workflow semantics primitives.

title: Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
text: Structural state intentionally minimal: dagri_recompute_state() knows whether node structurally ready blocked (upstream ready, pending gate way). Execution states like “done” “failed” belong whatever runtime build top.

title: Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
text: Planning pure query derives state internally — don’t need recompute first — external holds (reasons imposed caller, policy layer) affect plan without ever touching graph state: Resolving gate unblocks downstream node: graphs values, “changed?” never requires event log — keep old value compare:
code: library(dagriculture)  # 1. Define the kinds of nodes allowed in your graph registry <- dagri_registry(   dagri_kind("data_source"),   dagri_kind("transform") )  # 2. Build a graph; every verb returns a new value graph <- dagri_graph(registry) |>   dagri_add_node("raw_data", "data_source") |>   dagri_add_node("cleaned_data", "transform") |>   dagri_add_edge(from = "raw_data", to = "cleaned_data", id = "edge_1")  # 3. Gate the edge: downstream work is blocked until the gate resolves graph <- dagri_add_gate(graph, edge_id = "edge_1", id = "review_gate")  # 4. Compute structural state graph <- dagri_recompute_state(graph)  dagri_eligible(graph) #> [1] "raw_data"  dagri_blocked(graph) #> $cleaned_data #> [1] "gate" plan <- dagri_plan(   graph,   external_holds = list(cleaned_data = "manual_review") )  plan$topo_order #> [1] "raw_data"     "cleaned_data"  plan$external_blocked #> $cleaned_data #> [1] "manual_review"  plan$pending_gates #> [1] "review_gate" graph <- dagri_resolve_gate(graph, "review_gate") |>   dagri_recompute_state()  dagri_eligible(graph) #> [1] "raw_data"     "cleaned_data" before <- graph after <- dagri_add_node(graph, "report", "transform")  setdiff(names(after$nodes), names(before$nodes)) #> [1] "report"

title: Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
text: Beyond editing planning, package provides pure structural queries: dagri_upstream() / dagri_downstream() direct neighbors, dagri_ancestors() / dagri_descendants() reachability, dagri_roots() / dagri_leaves() / dagri_terminal() boundary nodes, dagri_topo_order() ordering, dagri_has_path() connectivity.

title: Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
text: workflow runner — nothing executes, caches, schedules. Pair runtime layer (see bayesgrove) write . general graph-theory package — targets typed task DAGs, arbitrary network analysis; use igraph . persistence layer — graphs serialize cleanly JSON plain data, reading writing caller’s job. contracts documented design/.

title: Pure Value-Oriented Directed Acyclic Graphs for Task Planning
text: design/ directory authoritative API surface boundary guarantees: api-contracts.md, boundary-contract.md, persistence-spec.md.

what: Build the internal adjacency index for a graph — dagri_adjacency
title: Build the internal adjacency index for a graph — dagri_adjacency
text: Performs single O(V+E) pass graph$edges returns four named lists keyed every node id names(graph$nodes) (initialized character(0) NULL-guarding needed):

title: Build the internal adjacency index for a graph — dagri_adjacency
Build the internal adjacency index for a graph — dagri_adjacency
text: graph dagri_graph.

title: Build the internal adjacency index for a graph — dagri_adjacency
text: named list components forward, reverse, forward_edges, reverse_edges, named list keyed node id.

title: Build the internal adjacency index for a graph — dagri_adjacency
text: forward: node -> unique vector downstream neighbor ids (edge$-> edge$) reverse: node -> unique vector upstream neighbor ids (edge$-> edge$) forward_edges: node -> vector outgoing edge ids (uniqued; edge distinct) reverse_edges: node -> vector incoming edge ids (uniqued) index derived per call never stored graph, pure-value, immutable public API unchanged. Internals previously re-scanned full edge list per neighbor lookup instead index pre-built maps.

title: Structural diff of two graphs — dagri_graph_diff
code: reg <- dagri_registry(dagri_kind("source"), dagri_kind("fit")) before <- dagri_graph(reg) |>   dagri_add_node("data", "source") |>   dagri_add_node("fit", "fit") |>   dagri_add_edge("data", "fit", id = "e1") after <- dagri_add_node(before, "diag", "fit") diff <- dagri_graph_diff(before, after) diff$added_nodes #> [1] "diag"

what: Render a dagriculture graph as Mermaid flowchart text — dagri_mermaid
title: Render a dagriculture graph as Mermaid flowchart text — dagri_mermaid
text: Emits Mermaid flowchart single length-1 character scalar embedded newlines (\n). output literal flowchart block — paste Mermaid renderer wrap fenced code block Markdown.

title: Render a dagriculture graph as Mermaid flowchart text — dagri_mermaid
code: dagri_mermaid(graph, node_label = NULL, node_class = NULL, direction = "TD")

title: Render a dagriculture graph as Mermaid flowchart text — dagri_mermaid
text: graph dagri_graph. node_label NULL function (node) -> string supplies displayed label node. Defaults function(node) node$label %||% node$id (use id node label). node_class NULL function (node) -> string supplies Mermaid CSS class name node. Defaults function(node) node$state %||% NA_character_; function returns NA, class line emitted node. direction Single character string, Mermaid flowchart direction. Defaults "TD" (top-). Common alternatives "LR", "RL", "BT".

title: Render a dagriculture graph as Mermaid flowchart text — dagri_mermaid
text: length-1 character scalar containing full Mermaid flowchart block, lines separated \n. empty graph yields just header line (e.g. "flowchart TD\n").

title: Render a dagriculture graph as Mermaid flowchart text — dagri_mermaid
text: Output shape. first line flowchart <direction>. Node lines follow (one per node names(graph$nodes) insertion order):   , non-NA class string produced, companion line:   Edge lines follow node/class lines:   Edges carrying one pending gates annotated gate ids joined ", ", e.g.   <> -- "gate: g1, g2" --> <>. Resolved gates produce annotation. Sanitization. Mermaid breaks ", (, ), [, ], {, }, |, <, >, embedded newlines. Node labels gate annotation text sanitized: " becomes ' characters become single spaces (runs whitespace collapsed trimmed). node_label / node_class return value NULL, NA, multi-element, non-character coerced length-1 character string (becomes "" coercion yields NA). Node ids sanitized. emitted verbatim Mermaid node identifiers, MUST Mermaid-safe (alphanumeric / underscore safe). Callers building graphs untrusted sources must validate ids rendering. Direction passed verbatim. direction's type validated (single non-NA non-empty string); value checked allowlist, unknown value emitted -Mermaid fail render .

title: Render a dagriculture graph as Mermaid flowchart text — dagri_mermaid
code: graph <- dagri_graph(dagri_registry(dagri_kind("source"), dagri_kind("fit"))) graph <- dagri_add_node(graph, "data", "source", label = "Data") graph <- dagri_add_node(graph, "fit", "fit", label = "Fit") graph <- dagri_add_edge(graph, "data", "fit", id = "e1") cat(dagri_mermaid(graph)) #> flowchart TD #>   data["Data"] #>   class data new #>   fit["Fit"] #>   class fit new #>   data --> fit

title: Get topological order — dagri_topo_order
text: O(V+E). Cycles detected : Kahn loop, node emitted (.e. still non-zero -degree) participates cycle, function aborts class dagri_error_cycle, naming cycle-participating nodes details$cycle_nodes. dagri_add_edge() prevents cycles edit time, consumers (e.g. bayesgrove) deserialize graphs JSON, corrupted hand-edited file can smuggle cycle past editing layer; check ensures planner never silently drops cycle-locked nodes order.

code: dagri_update_node(graph, id, label = NULL, params = NULL, metadata = NULL)

title: Update a node in a dagriculture graph — dagri_update_node
text: graph dagri_graph. id Node ID. label Node label. NULL leaves field unchanged. params Node parameters. non-NULL, replaces existing params outright (see Details). metadata Node metadata. non-NULL, replaces existing metadata outright (see Details).

title: Update a node in a dagriculture graph — dagri_update_node
text: params (non-NULL) replaces node's params outright; merged existing params. Likewise metadata (non-NULL) replaces node's metadata outright. merge partial updates, caller, e.g. dagri_update_node(graph, id, params = utils::modifyList(old_params, new_params)). NULL params/metadata/label leaves field untouched. Keeping primitive (replace, merge) means consumers (e.g. bayesgrove's bg_update_node()) must opt merge explicitly, instead silently partial update destroy sibling fields.

what: dagriculture: A pure, value-oriented DAG library — dagriculture-package
title: dagriculture: A pure, value-oriented DAG library — dagriculture-package
text: directed acyclic graph library managing structural dependencies, topological validity, task planning. Graphs immutable — every mutating function returns new graph instance bumps internal version counter.

title: dagriculture: A pure, value-oriented DAG library — dagriculture-package
text: Maintainer: Maximilian Scholz dev.scholz@mailbox.org Authors: Maximilian Scholz dev.scholz@mailbox.org

title: Print a dagriculture graph — print.dagri_graph
text: Prints concise multi-line summary dagri_graph: package name version, node/edge/gate counts, graph's internal $version, registry kind names. Matches style base R print methods (writes stdout via cat() trailing newlines).

title: dagriculture 0.3.0
text: dagri_plan() now derives node state internally local copy graph, returned eligible/blocked always current even input graph never passed dagri_recompute_state(). input graph value mutated.

title: dagriculture 0.3.0
text: dagri_ancestors(), dagri_descendants(), dagri_upstream(), dagri_downstream(), dagri_has_path() now abort dagri_error_not_found unknown node ids (previously returned silent empties FALSE). Id-taking public arguments (id///edge_id/name) now reject vectors, NA, empty strings dagri_error_invalid_argument instead dying base-R “condition length > 1” error. dagri_add_gate(graph, edge, ...)’s edge parameter renamed edge_id consistency rest API. Update named call sites. abort_dagri(), dagri_target_closure(), dagri_pending_gates() longer exported; results reachable dagri_plan()’s targets pending_gates fields. dagri_topo_order() dagri_terminal() longer accept index argument; build adjacency index . index type public constructor, threading public API footgun.

title: dagriculture 0.3.0
text: Minimum R version raised 4.4.0; %||% relies base R, provides since 4.4.0.

title: dagriculture 0.2.0
text: Cycle detection dagri_topo_order(): Kahn’s algorithm aborts dagri_error_cycle (details$cycle_nodes) instead silently returning partial order; closes hole graphs deserialized JSON. dagri_update_node() replace semantics documented: params/metadata replace existing fields outright (use utils::modifyList() merge); merge belongs consumer, primitive. Referential integrity dagri_validate_graph(): dangling edge /gate edge_id references abort dagri_error_invalid_argument; guards data loaded disk. Graph boundary helpers: added dagri_incoming_edges(), dagri_outgoing_edges(), dagri_order_edges(), dagri_edge_ids(), dagri_graph_diff() (pure structural diff). Mermaid flowchart export: added dagri_mermaid() — pure graph--text renderer injectable label/class functions pending-gate annotations. Print methods S3 classes: dagri_graph() dagri_plan() stamp S3 classes (still plain lists underneath) gain print.dagri_graph() / print.dagri_plan() summaries. Getting-started vignette: added Structural State Semantics section. Real package Title DESCRIPTION. Decision record — generic execution layer: documented execution, caching, artifact storage stay dagriculture (see design/boundary-contract.md).

title: dagriculture 0.2.0
text: Internal adjacency index: dagri_adjacency() builds forward/reverse neighbor maps one O(V+E) pass; traversals planning thread , taking walks O(V*E) O(V+E). Single-node dagri_upstream() / dagri_downstream() keep O(E) scan. Re-validation collapse: dagri_has_path() now runs downstream walk inline via shared index, instead delegating dagri_descendants() (re-built index). dagri_plan() builds one shared index across dagri_topo_order() dagri_external_blocked() calls. public boundary still cheap structural validation scans edge list per call.

title: dagriculture 0.1.6
text: Error message consistency: dagri_validate_node_ids(), dagri_validate_external_holds(), dagri_pending_gates() now use sprintf embed missing IDs error messages, matching pattern used -found errors. Package metadata: added explicit Author Maintainer fields DESCRIPTION bare R CMD check compatibility alongside existing Authors@R field. Build ignore cleanup: added .desloppify, .factory, .Rcheck, ..Rcheck .Rbuildignore.

title: dagriculture 0.1.5
text: param_schema validation: dagri_kind() now validates param_schema named list NULL scanning closures, rejecting non-lists unnamed inputs early. DFS re-validation: dagri_dfs() dagri_topo_order() longer re-validate graph every iteration, improving performance large reachability queries. Subset validation dagri_topo_order(): subset argument now validated de-duplicated via dagri_validate_node_ids() building topological sort. Documentation: fixed edge_id parameter name edge dagri_add_gate() calls README doc vignette. Expanded dagri_validate_graph() description document full validation scope.

title: dagriculture 0.1.4
text: Graph validation: public functions now validate graph argument required structure (dagri_validate_graph()), producing clear error instead cryptic R failures malformed input. Input contract enforcement: dagri_kind() validates input_contract named list character NULL values. dagri_add_node() checks node params satisfy kind’s input_contract creation time.

title: dagriculture 0.1.3
text: CI structural helper linting: fixed .lintr snapshot exclusions current lintr, documented dagri_target_closure() dagri_pending_gates(), updated dagri_terminal() docs scoped targets, shortened structural test fixture names satisfy CI lint checks.

title: dagriculture 0.1.2
text: Node deletion cleanup: dagri_remove_node() now removes incident edges gates attached edges, preventing dangling references structural deletes.

title: dagriculture 0.1.1
text: Planner-visible external holds: dagri_plan() now accepts caller-supplied external_holds, preserves structural eligible semantics, returns propagated non-structural holds external_blocked without mutating graph.

title: dagriculture 0.1.0
text: Constructors: Functions create define kinds, registries, core graph structure (dagri_kind(), dagri_registry(), dagri_graph()). Graph Editing: Implemented structural modifiers add/remove/update nodes, edges, explicit blockers called gates. Topology Queries: Topological sorting traversal utilities (ancestors, descendants, upstream, downstream, roots, leaves, reachability). Structural Planning: Determine node eligibility blocked reasons declarative resolution without side-effects executing runtime jobs (dagri_recompute_state(), dagri_plan()).

title: dagriculture 0.1.0
text: structures fully align plain-data spec serialized purely nested named lists (JSON compatible). Defined explicit typed errors abort_dagri(). Removed hello() template code.

title: dagriculture 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version.
